tweets.text:
The Morning Minute (7.13)

Powered by @yeet 

⏰Top News:
-Crypto majors fall 1-3% after overnight selloff; BTC at $63k
-BTC ETFs flip to net inflows after brutal 2-month stretch
-NEAR activated its v2.13.0 mainnet upgrade
-Zcash confirmed July 28 as the date for its Ironwood upgrade
-Robinhood Chain sees $2B+ in DEX volume over weekend with over 800k active addresses

🌎 Macro Crypto and Markets
-Crypto majors are down 1-3% after an overnight selloff; BTC -1% at $63k; ETH -1% at $1,780; SOL -1% at $76; HYPE -3% at $65
-JTO (+13%), PUMP (+8%) and PYTH (+6%) led top movers; ARB (+18%) and ZEC (+16%) were biggest winners of the week
-Oil +3.5% at $74; Gold -1% at $4,070
-Stock futures are red as the Iran War escalates again; DOW flat, Nasdaq -0.9%

-Bitcoin’s BIP-110 soft fork faces an early-August deadline with miner support at zero, the proposal to cap non-financial data on the chain never having drawn above 1%

-Meta’s chief data officer called agentic commerce the potential “next tier of business” for the company, saying stablecoins are central to a future without physical wallets, with over a million weekly active businesses already using Meta’s AI agents

Corporate Treasuries & ETFs
-The Bitcoin ETFs saw $197M in net inflows last week including $90M on Friday; the ETH ETFs saw $84M in inflows, including $18M on Friday

Meme Coin Tracker
-Meme leaders were mostly red on the week down 5-10%; DOGE -6%, SHIB -4%, PEPE +2%, PENGU -10%, TRUMP -7%, BONK -16%
-Robinhood chain memes rebounded after a weekend selloff; Cashcat -6% to $160M, Juggernaut +50% to $15M, Hoodrat +45% to $12M and RMI +220x to $7M
-ANSEM rebounded 25% to $250M leading Solana movers; TripleT +20% and febu +60% were other notable Solana movers

💰 Token, Airdrop & Protocol Tracker
-Robinhood Chain sees over $2B in DEX volume over the weekend with over 800k active addresses
-NEAR activated its v2.13.0 mainnet upgrade, adding post-quantum signature support, automatic shard scaling, and a redesigned gas payment system
-Zcash confirmed July 28 as the date for its Ironwood upgrade
-Backpack launched 24/7 trading of tokenized US stocks

🚚 What is happening in NFTs?
-NFT leaders were mixed; Punks even at 32.4 ETH, BAYC -4% at 8.9 ETH, Pudgy -3% at 4.4 ETH; Hypurr’s -6% at 287 HYPE
-Murakami Flowers (+23%) and Squiggles (+13%) led top movers
-New Robinhood NFT sets jumped including Robbin Hood Babies (+160%) and Onchain Hoodies (+27%)

tweets.text:
"For most of history, the bottleneck on making something was never the idea, it was the grind: acquiring the years of skill, raising the money, assembling the team, and getting the permission. So most people’s best ideas died inside them, unmade.

Lift that bottleneck and the thing that decides what gets built is no longer whether people can justify the VC funding or the enterprise-level capital expenditure, but who has something to say.

The most human thing about you stops being a private quirk and starts being the point.

Individuality was supposed to be the luxury you bought once you’d made it. I think it’s about to become the thing everyone gets to spend their life on, the work itself."

a16z GP Anish Acharya on why AI is the most human technology ever made: https://t.co/uUmkxHOQuc

Lift that bottleneck and the thing that decides what gets built is no longer whether people can justify the VC funding or the enterprise-level capital expenditure, but who has something to say.

The most human thing about you stops being a private quirk and starts being the point.

Individuality was supposed to be the luxury you bought once you’d made it. I think it’s about to become the thing everyone gets to spend their life on, the work itself."

tweets.text:
BREAKING: Total US federal debt is now up to a record $39.4 trillion, rising +$3.2 trillion over the last 12 months.

Since 2020, US federal debt is now up a massive +$16.3 trillion.

This marks a +$2.5 trillion average annual increase, or +$209 billion per month.

At this pace, total US debt will surge to $50.0 trillion before 2030.

The US debt crisis has no end in sight.

tweets.text: Love this site but one tweak I’d make: make the algo less sensitive less over-zealous. If you spend an extra second on a post next thing you know half of your TL is that specific topic. I think it should wait till you show interest 3-4x in something bf fetching content and default more to posts from ppl you follow.

tweets.text: Leveraged ETFs have been catching a lot of crap lately as the new boogeyman du jour. I get it but to some degree their impact is overblown. They make up 34% of the launches 13% of launches (and much of media coverage) so they can seem massive but they only acct for 1% of ETF assets and an even smaller % of US equity mkts. That said, they are risky and vol drag can be a nasty surprise. But most people know this and trade them (hence the high volume to aum ratio) and day to day they largely do a great job of tracking. I equate them to fast food and whiskey. They aren't healthy but they are legal and they can deliver the hit you looking for. Just gotta understand the risks. Also I found something shocking... (next post)

thread_tweets.text: So I went in to look at how much cash they've burned over their life and I was going to compare this to the $750b active MFs have burned in underperformance to put that # into perspective but when i ran numbers I found they actually GENERATED $66b in cash for the traders that use them. Many of them like $TQQQ are up a TON (38,000%) but yet have seen outflows (traders taking profits) and this is net of vol drag and includes -2x funds. Here's the biggest generators and biggest burners. And this is why they remain popular despite the high priests admonishing them every other day.
thread_tweets.full_text: So I went in to look at how much cash they've burned over their life and I was going to compare this to the $750b active MFs have burned in underperformance to put that # into perspective but when i ran numbers I found they actually GENERATED $66b in cash for the traders that use them. Many of them like $TQQQ are up a TON (38,000%) but yet have seen outflows (traders taking profits) and this is net of vol drag and includes -2x funds. Here's the biggest generators and biggest burners. And this is why they remain popular despite the high priests admonishing them every other day.

thread_tweets.text: Now, would want my mom using these ETFs? No. But I wouldn't want her chugging Jack Daniels either. This is why our solution is our Traffic Light system. Modeled off of movie ratings. Green = PG, Yellow = PG-13 and Red = R. All leveraged ETFs are immediate Red light. This is how to let degens (or reg investors' cowboy accounts) do their thing - which I'm not going to judge them for - while protecting the innocent.

I equate them to fast food and whiskey. They aren't healthy but they are legal and they can deliver the hit you looking for. Just gotta understand the risks. Also I found something shocking... (next post)

So I went in to look at how much cash they've burned over their life and I was going to compare this to the $750b active MFs have burned in underperformance to put that # into perspective but when i ran numbers I found they actually GENERATED $66b in cash for the traders that use them. Many of them like $TQQQ are up a TON (38,000%) but yet have seen outflows (traders taking profits) and this is net of vol drag and includes -2x funds. Here's the biggest generators and biggest burners. And this is why they remain popular despite the high priests admonishing them every other day.

Now, would want my mom using these ETFs? No. But I wouldn't want her chugging Jack Daniels either. This is why our solution is our Traffic Light system. Modeled off of movie ratings. Green = PG, Yellow = PG-13 and Red = R. All leveraged ETFs are immediate Red light. This is how to let degens (or reg investors' cowboy accounts) do their thing - which I'm not going to judge them for - while protecting the innocent.

thread_tweets.text:
Rent control is great politics and terrible economics.

It rewards existing tenants while making housing scarcer and more expensive for the next generation.

You can cap rents, but you can’t repeal supply and demand.

Brazilian stocks finally brought friends.

For 52 sessions, fewer than half of Ibovespa stocks traded above their 50-day averages.
After similar breaks, local currency returns were positive every time over the next 1 to 5 months, with no drawdown worse than 5%.

For $EWZ, currency swings have meant double-digit drawdowns.

Read full analysis: https://t.co/wX5yLsoGWV
